FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TextEditCore

Namespace
FluentGpu.Text
Assembly
FluentGpu.Text.dll

The portable text-editing state machine: an EditDocument + caret/selection + bounded undo, with WinUI TextBox semantics made exact. Pure C# over the BCL — no PAL/Scene/Render types — so the same core drives TextBox/PasswordBox/NumberBox/AutoSuggestBox/ComboBox on Windows AND headless checks.

Contracts every mutation upholds: caret positions are grapheme boundaries (never inside a surrogate pair, ZWJ emoji, or combining sequence); Anchor/Active stay in [0, Length]; every document change bumps Doc.Version; undo/redo restore the EXACT before/after caret+anchor pairs. Caret movement and selection ops allocate nothing — strings materialize only at the user-gesture edge (copy, undo capture, paste normalization).

public sealed class TextEditCore
Inheritance
object
TextEditCore
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)

object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Fields

StickyX

The Up/Down goal column in layout DIPs. The CONTROL owns it (only the control has visual metrics): it reads the value when applying Up/Down and writes it back after the vertical move. The core's only obligation is to reset it to NaN on every horizontal move and edit, so a stale column never teleports the caret.

public float StickyX

Field Value

float

Properties

AcceptsReturn

When false (single-line), InsertText(ReadOnlySpan<char>) keeps only the FIRST line of incoming text (WinUI's paste flattening) and InsertNewline no-ops.

public bool AcceptsReturn { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

Active

The moving end of the selection — the caret.

public int Active { get; }

Property Value

int

Anchor

The fixed end of the selection (where Shift-selection started). Equals Active when the selection is empty.

public int Anchor { get; }

Property Value

int

CanRedo 

public bool CanRedo { get; }

Property Value

bool

CanUndo 

public bool CanUndo { get; }

Property Value

bool

Doc

The owned document. Callers may read it freely; all writes must go through this core so caret, undo, and grapheme invariants hold (the raw document does not know about any of them).

public EditDocument Doc { get; }

Property Value

EditDocument

HasSelection 

public bool HasSelection { get; }

Property Value

bool

IsReadOnly

When true, all mutations no-op (including undo/redo) but caret movement, selection, and copy keep working — WinUI's read-only TextBox.

public bool IsReadOnly { get; set; }

Property Value

bool

MaxLength

Maximum length in UTF-16 code units; 0 = unlimited (WinUI MaxLength). Enforced on insert and paste by clamping the INSERTED text (snapped down to a grapheme boundary so a clamp can never split a cluster); existing content is never truncated by setting this.

public int MaxLength { get; set; }

Property Value

int

SelectedText

The selected text ("" when collapsed). Allocates — user-gesture edge only.

public string SelectedText { get; }

Property Value

string

Selection

The selection normalized to (min, length) regardless of drag direction.

public (int Start, int Length) Selection { get; }

Property Value

(int Start, int End)

UndoDepth

Observable undo depth (headless coalescing checks: typing "abc" leaves exactly 1).

public int UndoDepth { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

Apply(TextEditCommand, bool)

Execute a document-order command; returns true iff caret/selection/document state changed. Up/Down/PageUp/ PageDown/Copy/Cut/Paste/Commit/Cancel always return false — the CONTROL layer handles them (they need visual metrics or the clipboard seam). WinUI arrow contract: Left/Right with a selection and no Shift COLLAPSE the caret to the selection's edge WITHOUT moving it; only a collapsed caret moves by grapheme.

public bool Apply(TextEditCommand cmd, bool extend = false)

Parameters

cmd TextEditCommand
extend bool

Returns

bool

ClearUndoHistory() 

public void ClearUndoHistory()

CopySelection()

The text to copy, or null when nothing is selected. Copy works in read-only fields.

public string? CopySelection()

Returns

string

CutSelection()

Copy + remove the selection through the undo path (its own sealed undo step). Null when nothing is selected or the field is read-only (a read-only cut must not even return the text — WinUI treats it as copy via the Copy command instead).

public string? CutSelection()

Returns

string

InsertText(ReadOnlySpan<char>)

Insert at the caret, replacing any selection as a single undo op. Respects IsReadOnly; flattens to the first line when AcceptsReturn is false (so pasting multi-line text into a single-line field keeps only line one, exactly like WinUI — note an empty flattened paste still replaces the selection); clamps against MaxLength at a grapheme boundary. Single-grapheme inserts at a collapsed caret push coalescable Typing ops (a typing burst undoes in one step); everything else seals. Returns true iff the document changed.

public bool InsertText(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>

Returns

bool

Paste(ReadOnlySpan<char>)

Paste = seal + InsertText(ReadOnlySpan<char>) + seal: the paste is always its own undo step, and later typing never merges into it — even a one-character paste.

public bool Paste(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>

Returns

bool

ResetText(ReadOnlySpan<char>)

Programmatic external write (the Text property setter): replaces all content (newlines normalized to '\r'), CLEARS the undo history (WinUI does — a programmatic set is not a user edit to step back over), and collapses the selection to min(old caret, new length), grapheme-snapped.

public void ResetText(ReadOnlySpan<char> text)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>

Select(int, int)

Programmatic selection (WinUI Select(start, length)): both ends clamped and grapheme-snapped; the caret lands at the selection end. Seals the undo run.

public void Select(int start, int length)

Parameters

start int
length int

SelectAll() 

public void SelectAll()

SetCaret(int, bool)

Place the caret (pointer click / programmatic). Clamps to [0, Length] and snaps DOWN to a grapheme boundary. extend keeps Anchor (Shift+click); otherwise the selection collapses. Caret relocation seals the undo run (typing after a click is a new undo step) and resets StickyX.

public void SetCaret(int pos, bool extend = false)

Parameters

pos int
extend bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
